_comment: Pin down / stall demo: a lone player Infantry regiment (scenario spawn, count 60 so the clash resolves within a modest frame budget) faces a lone enemy Infantry regiment spawned close by and already advancing -- a clean, isolated head-on confrontation with nothing else on the field to confound the read (see CLAUDE.md/sparta-demos.md on isolating the phenomenon in question). The player unit is selected and given the Pin down stance (Semicolon) before the enemy AI's approach closes to contact, then an explicit attack order locks the engagement. Once in contact, the pinning unit swings roughly half as often as normal (PIN_DOWN_ATTACK_INTERVAL, 1.2s = 72 ticks, vs. ATTACK_INTERVAL's 0.6s = 36 ticks) and each swing opens a brief exposure window (PIN_DOWN_EXPOSURE_DURATION, 0.3s = 18 ticks) during which its own defense is scaled down (PIN_DOWN_DEFENSE_FACTOR). Verified via tools/demo/dump-state.sh at tick granularity: contact lands at tick 137 (the player's first own strike -- the enemy strikes one tick earlier, at 136), opening the exposure window through tick 155; the player's second own strike lands at tick 280 (roughly two PIN_DOWN_ATTACK_INTERVAL cycles later -- an intervening cycle around tick 209 is skipped because the two full-width lines slowly rotate against each other after contact, a pre-existing engagement-turn dynamic unrelated to PIN_DOWN, which briefly widens the facing offset past the fight-tolerance threshold). The state ticks below bracket the first strike/exposure window precisely and sample the following swings loosely enough to tolerate that drift.
